name: delegate_to_agent
description: Hand off a self-contained sub-task to a separate local AI agent that runs in its own session. ASYNCHRONOUS: returns a task_id right away and the sub-agent keeps working in the background — this call never blocks. So you can fan out several delegations at once and keep working, then collect each result later with get_delegation_status(task_id), or stop one early with cancel_delegation(task_id). The sub-agent CANNOT see this conversation, your open files, or earlier turns — it starts cold, so `task` must carry everything it needs. Best for independent, parallelizable work you can describe up front; not for steps that need your ongoing back-and-forth.

agent_type.description: Which local agent runs the sub-task. Pick the one best suited to the work.

task.description: The complete, self-contained prompt for the sub-agent. It has no access to this conversation or your context, so spell out everything: the goal, the relevant background and absolute file paths, any constraints, and exactly what to return.

working_dir.description: Absolute path the sub-agent runs in. Defaults to this session's working directory.

name: get_delegation_status
description: Collect a delegated task's result — or check on it — by the task_id from delegate_to_agent. The wait_ms argument picks the mode: omit it for a non-blocking snapshot (poll), or pass wait_ms to wait for a terminal state — wait_ms>0 blocks up to that many ms (capped at 60000; re-call to keep waiting), wait_ms=0 blocks with no timeout for as long as the sub-agent runs. When you just need the result, prefer blocking over repeated polling. Reports the current state — running, or a terminal completed / failed / canceled (or unknown if the id isn't recognized). A finished task includes its full result text.

wait_ms.description: How long to wait for the task to finish. Omit for an immediate snapshot (poll). A positive value blocks up to that many milliseconds (capped at 60000). 0 means no timeout — block until the task reaches a terminal state, however long it takes.

name: cancel_delegation
description: Stop a running delegated task by its task_id and tear down its sub-agent — use this only when you no longer want the result. Don't cancel just because a task is taking a while: substantial work can legitimately run for a long time, so prefer waiting on it (get_delegation_status with wait_ms) over canceling. If the task has already finished, nothing is canceled and its final result is returned instead.
